behavior_summary: U8P_BOTTLE::look: When the player examines the bottle item, the script reads the item's frame (Item::getFrame(this)) and displays a short human-readable label for that drink. Known frames map to specific drink names (Blackwine, Hurricane, Breath o' Spirit, Cloven Hoof, Tenebraen Ale); other frames display a generic 'bottle'.

U8P_BOTTLE::use: Handles drinking the bottle. It checks the bottle quantity (Item::getQ(this)), marks the bottle consumed (Item::setQ), applies a FeedAvatar effect whose magnitude/type depends on the bottle frame, and advances a global intoxication state 'gettingDrunk'. If intoxication reaches its final stage, the function triggers an extended drunken sequence that spawns visual/animation processes, temporarily puts the avatar in stasis, emits a long bark ('Zzzzz...') and then resets 'gettingDrunk'. If the bottle is empty the player is shown 'It's empty...'.

U8P_BOTTLE::func0562: Initializes and runs the early drunkenness routine. It sets gettingDrunk to the initial state, spawns helper visual/process effects, conditionally emits a drunken bark ('hic...') when nobody else is talking, and manages internal counters and spawn bookkeeping used by the longer drunken sequence.
notable_control_flow: Examine flow is simple: read item frame -> select bark -> suspend (end).

Use/drink flow branches on Item::getQ(this) (empty vs drinkable). For a drinkable bottle it: setQ(0x01), FeedAvatar(...) with frame-dependent values, then either start drunkenness (call U8P_BOTTLE::func0562) if gettingDrunk==0 or increment gettingDrunk through states 1→2→3. When gettingDrunk reaches the terminal state a multi-step sequence runs involving resetRef, setAvatarInStasis(1), many spawn/suspend calls, and finally resetting gettingDrunk to 0.

Drunkenness initializer (U8P_BOTTLE::func0562) contains a loop/counter that increments local counters, uses urandom/Npc::getDir to pick directions for spawned effects, and rolls the gettingDrunk state over time; reaching thresholds triggers state transitions and end-of-sequence actions (including 'Burp...' barks and ref resets).
open_questions: Exact numeric FeedAvatar effects per bottle frame: Scout data gives differing numeric args but the precise in-game effect (HP, stamina, temporary buff) is inferred, not canonical.
Which engine reference slots (ref_0x020A, ref_0x0200) correspond to which subsystems (visuals, audio, or process bookkeeping) — their numeric ids and purpose are inferred from usage.
Precise visual/audio content of the spawned FREE::func1C19 / FREE::func29D8 and addressof(...)->METHOD::func15E7 processes — code shows spawns but not the spawned function bodies here.

main_function.name: U8P_BOTTLE::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown when the player examines the bottle; map item frame to a drink name or a generic label.
entry_conditions: Triggered by an examine/U8P_BOTTLE::look action on the bottle item (U8P_BOTTLE::look process type 0x0208).
Reads the implicit item instance 'this' via Item::getFrame(this) to determine which label to display.

function_map.name: U8P_BOTTLE::look
function_map.role: examine handler that maps Item::getFrame(this) to a short label

function_map.name: U8P_BOTTLE::use
function_map.role: U8P_BOTTLE::use/drink handler that consumes quantity, applies FeedAvatar effects, and advances/controls the global intoxication state

function_map.name: U8P_BOTTLE::func0562
function_map.role: drunkenness initializer and ongoing drunkenness routine that spawns effects, manages counters, and sets gettingDrunk

internal_calls.purpose: Start the drunkenness routine when gettingDrunk is not already set (initialize/gettingDrunk=1).

external_calls.purpose: Read the bottle's frame/type to choose a human-readable label.
Determine which FeedAvatar argument/value to apply based on bottle type.
Check whether the bottle has quantity (is drinkable) or is empty.
Mark the bottle as consumed/adjust its quantity after drinking.
Apply immediate benefits/effects (nutrition/HP/stamina-like) to the avatar; numeric arg depends on frame.
Set/clear engine reference slots (e.g., ref_0x020A, ref_0x0200) during the final drunken sequence for bookkeeping/cleanup.
Temporarily disable avatar control during the extended drunken sequence.
Spawn helper/visual processes used in the drunken animation sequence (timed effects).
Randomly decide whether to emit a drunken bark ('hic...') and to vary spawned effect parameters.
Obtain an NPC/avatar facing to compute directions for spawned animations/effects.
Spawn/trigger directional animation/effect calls on NPCs/objects using computed directions.
Branch spawned effects/animations based on NPC animation state and busy status.

reads.name: somebodyTalking
reads.likely_effect: Suppress barks/effects when another conversation is active to avoid overlapping speech.

reads.name: gettingDrunk
reads.likely_effect: Read to determine current intoxication progression and whether to start/advance the drunkenness routine.

writes.name: gettingDrunk
writes.likely_effect: Tracks intoxication state: initialized to 1 by U8P_BOTTLE::func0562, incremented by U8P_BOTTLE::use, and reset to 0 when the extended drunken sequence completes.

writes.name: ref_0x020A
writes.likely_effect: Engine reference cleared/updated as part of the drunken sequence cleanup/initialization (inferred bookkeeping slot).

writes.name: ref_0x0200
writes.likely_effect: Engine reference set during the drunken sequence (inferred as another bookkeeping/visual slot).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_BOTTLE::look: When the player examines the bottle item, the script reads the item's frame (Item::getFrame(this)) and displays a short human-readable label for that drink. Known frames map to specific drink names (Blackwine, Hurricane, Breath o' Spirit, Cloven Hoof, Tenebraen Ale); other frames display a generic 'bottle'.
